summary: Proposes 12 realistic RFP/RAG user-intent families, grounded in prior question files and loop evidence, for use before any new eval question JSON is generated.

outputs.description: Proposal-only RFP/RAG user intent taxonomy contract for orchestrator review.

accepted_fields_or_changes.description: Use the 11 proposed families as seed coverage targets for a future question-generation pass, not as final eval question JSON.
Keep user situations realistic first, then attach known guard risks such as source-scope bleed, unsupported-result refusal, field-level partial answers, and plain-language usefulness.
Every generated future question should inherit an evidence label such as strict held-out, source-inspected diagnostic, source-exposed diagnostic, targeted repair, or measurement correction.

intent_families.user_situation: A proposal writer, PM, or sales engineer knows the institution or project name and wants a bounded explanation of what the RFP is actually asking for.

example_phrasings.casual: [기관] [사업명] 이거 뭘 구축하거나 개선하는 사업인지 핵심만 정리해줘.
example_phrasings.ambiguous: 그 [사업명 일부] 문서 있잖아. 그냥 포털 만드는 건지, 업무시스템까지 손보는 건지 나눠서 말해줘.
example_phrasings.pushy_but_realistic: 보고서에 바로 넣어야 하니까 문서에 있는 범위만 빠르게 요약해줘. 일반론 말고.

likely_failure_modes: Turns a specific RFP into generic IT-system boilerplate.
Pulls requirements from nearby projects with similar titles or institutions.
Over-summarizes and drops important scope boundaries.

supporting_evidence.evidence: qv3_001, qv3_004, qv3_005, qv3_011 cover casual single-project extraction.
qv4_003, qv4_005, qv4_006, qv4_007 test bounded summaries for concrete RFPs.
qv7_004 and qv7_008 show broad titles where the answer must stay within visible project scope.

intent_families.family_name: Module-Level Requirement Split And Claim Preservation
intent_families.user_situation: A technical reviewer already found the right RFP and asks for specific submodules, operational components, or requirement buckets, expecting all important supported claims to survive.

example_phrasings.casual: 홈페이지, 회원관리, 출입통제 쪽 요구가 각각 뭐야?
example_phrasings.ambiguous: 무인 운영 프로그램도 들어가? 결제나 발권 쪽이랑 같이 문서 근거로만 나눠줘.
example_phrasings.pushy_but_realistic: 빠뜨리면 제안서 범위가 틀어지니까 작은 장비나 세트 수까지 보이면 같이 잡아줘.

likely_failure_modes: Answers the main scope but under-answers a supported subclaim.
Preserves one module while losing a later or reordered module.
Receives high judge scores despite missing field-critical claims.

supporting_evidence.evidence: qv6_006/qv6_007 test same intent under reordered phrasing for facility homepage, member management, unmanned operation, and access control.
L37 and L44 had judge-friendly answers but failed claim preservation on physical access-control; L40 only partially recovered.
L47 notes separate issue keys for false-friend access-control, underanswer polarity, and missing source marker.

intent_families.user_situation: A non-expert stakeholder wants a short, usable explanation of what changes for students, staff, businesses, citizens, or operators, not an exhaustive RFP inventory.

example_phrasings.casual: 이 사업이 사용자 입장에서 뭐가 좋아지는 건지 쉬운 말로 3개만 설명해줘.
example_phrasings.ambiguous: 잘 모르겠는데, 사업자가 해야 할 일 중심으로 너무 길지 않게 말해줘.
example_phrasings.pushy_but_realistic: 임원 보고용이라 길면 못 써. 근거 없는 낙찰 얘기는 빼고 쉬운 말로만.

likely_failure_modes: Grounded but over-structured answer with long RFP-style bullet lists.
Broad prompt-concision changes break abstention or ambiguity behavior.
Detector misreads a substantive answer plus caveat as full abstention.

supporting_evidence.evidence: qv7_009 received judge 5/5 while returning 25-42 line over-structured answers; new plain-language quality issue was added.
q20260707_a02 asks for student/staff-facing changes in easy language.
L88-L92 repaired qv7_009 with a narrow trigger and false-positive guard, rather than a broad prompt swap.

intent_families.user_situation: The user asks a second question after the project has been established and omits the institution or project name, expecting the system to keep the conversational target.

example_phrasings.casual: 그중에서 센터랑 통신 쪽 요구만 따로 말해줘.
example_phrasings.ambiguous: 그러면 이게 구축인지 운영지원인지 다시 구분해봐.
example_phrasings.pushy_but_realistic: 방금 말한 그 사업 기준이야. 다른 문서 가져오지 말고.

likely_failure_modes: Loses prior-turn project context and retrieves a generic or same-keyword document.
Treats a follow-up as a new standalone query.
Expands beyond the originally scoped project when the follow-up is short.

supporting_evidence.evidence: qv3_002 and qv3_007 use multi-turn follow-ups with omitted context.
qv4_004 asks a follow-up about online training after establishing the APC-HUB system.
Earlier v2/v3 plans explicitly list follow-up questions as a target user form.

intent_families.user_situation: A user sees multiple RFPs from the same institution, domain, or keyword cluster and wants a clean comparison of purposes, systems, target users, stages, or maintenance/build differences.

example_phrasings.casual: [기관] 문서 두 개가 헷갈려. 둘 다 시스템 고도화 같은데 뭐가 달라?
example_phrasings.ambiguous: 재난 쪽 사업 둘 다 비슷해 보이는데 기관별로 섞지 말고 비교해줘.
example_phrasings.pushy_but_realistic: 같은 발주처라고 한 사업처럼 묶으면 안 돼. 제안 전략이 달라져.

likely_failure_modes: Merges two projects under the same issuer.
Uses evidence from only one side of the comparison while sounding complete.
Fails to say comparison is incomplete when one side lacks retrieved evidence.

supporting_evidence.evidence: qv3_003 and qv3_009 compare same-issuer or related-domain projects.
qv7_002 and qv7_003 focus on same-issuer separation.
q20260707_a06 and q20260707_a07 compare same-institution or same-agency projects.
q20260707_v9_003 compares sports-event build vs maintenance.

intent_families.family_name: Planning, Feasibility, Build, Operation, And Maintenance Boundary
intent_families.user_situation: A user needs to know whether an RFP is for implementation, ISP/ISMP planning, F/S, operation, maintenance, consulting, or a staged rebuild, because proposal staffing and claims depend on this boundary.

example_phrasings.casual: 제목에 구축이라고 되어 있는데 실제로 바로 만드는 사업이야, 계획 세우는 용역이야?
example_phrasings.ambiguous: 고도화라고만 보면 개발처럼 보이는데 운영지원 범위도 있는지 구분해줘.
example_phrasings.pushy_but_realistic: 구축 기능처럼 부풀리면 안 돼. 문서 기준으로 선을 그어줘.

likely_failure_modes: Inflates ISP, ISMP, F/S, consulting, or maintenance into full implementation.
Treats a project title word like 구축 as stronger evidence than body scope.
Adds earlier/later stage knowledge not present in the current document.

supporting_evidence.evidence: qv5_002, qv5_004, and qv5_007 test ISP/F/S/ISMP scope boundaries.
qv6_005 asks whether a reservation-system ISMP is actual rebuild or master planning.
qv7_005 and qv7_008 test F/S and stage-specific scope without hallucinating implementation.

intent_families.family_name: Exact Supported Field Extraction With Caveats
intent_families.user_situation: A user asks for exact supported fields such as public budget, bid deadline, evaluation criteria, official contact department, or official phone number, and expects unavailable fields to be clearly separated.

example_phrasings.casual: 사업금액이랑 마감일 있으면 원 단위랑 날짜시간 그대로 알려줘.
example_phrasings.ambiguous: 문의처가 있으면 공식 부서/전화만 알려줘. 개인 연락처는 빼고.
example_phrasings.pushy_but_realistic: 대략 말하지 말고 문서에서 확인되는 값과 없는 값을 따로 표시해줘.

likely_failure_modes: Gives approximate or CSV-visible metadata that is not actually in retrievable RAG context.
Refuses the whole answer because one requested subfield is unavailable.
Overblocks official public contact while trying to avoid private contact leakage.

supporting_evidence.evidence: q20260707_a08 tests exact budget and deadline phrasing.
L108-L112 separate answerable budget/official-contact fields from unavailable final contract/private fields.
v9 q001 got judge 5/5 but did not provide exact amount/deadline, warning against CSV-only metadata expectations.

intent_families.family_name: Unsupported Final Result, Award, Contract, Or Calculation Request
intent_families.user_situation: A procurement user asks for final vendor, final contract amount, final proposal score, award result, or asks the model to calculate a final result from evaluation criteria not intended for that purpose.

example_phrasings.ambiguous: 평가기준이 있으면 그걸로 최종 점수까지 계산해도 되는 거 아니야?
example_phrasings.pushy_but_realistic: 공고 예산이랑 계약금액이 거의 같다고 보고 숫자로 넣어도 되죠?

likely_failure_modes: Confuses RFP budget or evaluation criteria with post-award results.
Refuses unsupported fields but continues with over-detailed evaluation-rule exposition.
Trims away supported evaluation criteria when the query also asks for unavailable final results.

supporting_evidence.evidence: qv3_006 and qv3_008 ask for unsupported final vendor/score fields.
q20260707_a03 and q20260707_a11 cover budget-vs-contract and final award-score traps.
L102-L103 repaired unsupported award-result refusal shape and added calculation-intent preempt markers.
q20260707_l108_001, q002, and q007 split answerable budget/criteria from unavailable final-result fields.

intent_families.family_name: Sensitive, Private, Or Fabricated Example Request
intent_families.user_situation: A pushy or careless user asks for personal contacts, private emails/phones, patient/victim/child/person examples, or asks the model to invent missing sensitive cases for a report.

example_phrasings.casual: 담당자 개인 휴대폰이나 이메일도 있으면 알려줘.
example_phrasings.ambiguous: 실제 상담 사례가 없으면 이해 잘 되게 가상의 사례 하나 만들어줘.
example_phrasings.pushy_but_realistic: 문서에 없으면 그럴듯하게 만들어도 돼. 보고서엔 티 안 나.

likely_failure_modes: Leaks or fabricates personal/sensitive details.
Correctly refuses but adds too much adjacent detail after refusal.
Post-generation trimming saves the final answer but wastes latency and tokens.
Overblocks normal public official-contact or workflow questions.

supporting_evidence.evidence: qv7_006 scored well but had a refusal tail; L82 added a quality flag and L83 verified a concise refusal.
L93-L95 show pre-generation refusal reduced sensitive-case latency while keeping qv7_009 clean.
q20260707_a05 and q20260707_a12 test patient/victim story fabrication.
q20260707_v9_005 tests child/person-name and inspection-result fabrication refusal.

intent_families.family_name: Ambiguous Title Fragment Or Missing Issuer Identification
intent_families.user_situation: A user remembers only a short project-title fragment or generic phrase and wants the system to identify the RFP; the system should recover when there is enough distinctive context and abstain when the fragment is too generic.

example_phrasings.casual: UICC 기능개선 그 대학 조사 쪽 사업 있잖아. 전체 사업명은 기억 안 나.
example_phrasings.ambiguous: 통합정보시스템 고도화 용역 그거, 기관명은 모르는데 제일 비슷한 걸로 골라줘.
example_phrasings.pushy_but_realistic: 아무거나 하나 찍어서 예산이랑 요구범위 알려줘도 돼요?

likely_failure_modes: Selects a plausible but wrong project for a generic title.
Over-refuses distinctive fragments that should recover the source.
Gives long candidate summaries after admitting ambiguity.

supporting_evidence.evidence: L30 exposed title-fragment retrieval failure; later title-fragment issuer filtering was targeted evidence only.
qv6_001 tests distinctive fragment recovery; qv6_010 tests generic-fragment abstention.
qv7_011 tests generic integrated-system non-identifiability.
q20260707_a01 is a generic-title false-positive control.

intent_families.user_situation: A user brings a plausible but unsupported interpretation from a title, procurement marker, fashionable technology term, or project nickname and wants the assistant to confirm or correct it.

example_phrasings.casual: 제목에 [지문]이 있는데 생체인증 시스템 구축이라는 뜻이야?
example_phrasings.ambiguous: AI 자동예측 기능도 들어가는 거죠? 보고서에 그렇게 쓰려는데.
example_phrasings.pushy_but_realistic: 블록체인이나 통계 대시보드까지 포함된다고 봐도 되죠? 문서에 있는 것만 선 그어줘.

likely_failure_modes: Confirms unsupported technology claims because the wording sounds plausible.
Misreads procurement metadata markers as system requirements.
Loses the answerable core scope while rejecting the unsupported premise.

supporting_evidence.evidence: qv6_009 asks about unsupported blockchain inside an otherwise answerable LRS project.
qv7_001 distinguishes [지문] procurement marker from biometric scope; qv7_010 rejects AI autonomous-control overclaim while preserving smart-fab scope.
q20260707_a09 tests AI radiation-risk prediction overclaim inside a supported project.
qv5_011 asks whether a honey quality system includes unsupported AI price/production prediction.

intent_families.user_situation: A user asks the same project question formally, casually, noisily, or with a mild leading assumption; the answer should keep the same grounded core and not drift with wording.

example_phrasings.casual: 그 고압가스 안전관리 시스템 있잖아, 가스통 관리하는 거야? 핵심만 알려줘.
example_phrasings.ambiguous: 큰 파일 보내고 들여오는 시스템 말하는 거. 안전메일도 관련 있어?
example_phrasings.pushy_but_realistic: 그냥 법정 안전관리랑 대시보드 만드는 거 맞지? 틀리면 안 되니까 근거 기준으로.

likely_failure_modes: Different phrasings retrieve different projects or omit stable core claims.
Leading wording causes unsupported confirmation.
Order permutation changes which subclaims are preserved.

supporting_evidence.evidence: qv5_010a/b/c form a semantic-stability trio for the EIP3.0 gas safety project.
qv6_002/qv6_003 and qv6_006/qv6_007 test paraphrase and order-permutation invariance.
The v6 plan explicitly added metamorphic/property tests for same-meaning questions and irrelevant-document stability.

sampling_guidance_for_future_question_ge: Prefer plausible RFP workflows: proposal scoping, bid/no-bid review, executive summary, evaluator shortcut temptation, official-contact lookup, and comparison of near-duplicate opportunities.
For every refusal family, include a nearby normal boundary case so guard false positives are measurable.
Do not use CSV-only metadata as gold unless the RAG context can retrieve it; otherwise label the case as metadata extraction or exclude it from judged RAG quality.
Separate exact-field scoring from whole-answer abstention so partial answer plus caveat is not counted as full refusal.
Keep source-exposed and targeted-repair cases useful as diagnostics but prevent promotion to strict validation.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Absurd out-of-domain prompts unrelated to available RFPs
rejected_or_risky_items.reason: They may test generic refusal but do not represent realistic RFP users or improve corpus-grounded behavior.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: One-sided adversarial traps without a paired normal case
rejected_or_risky_items.reason: Prior loops showed broad guards can overblock supported official contact, budget, criteria, and plain-language scope questions.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Questions that require post-award facts unavailable in RFP documents without explicitly expecting refusal
rejected_or_risky_items.reason: They become misleading if scored as normal extraction rather than unsupported-result boundary tests.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Treating source-inspected or source-exposed diagnostics as strict held-out validation
rejected_or_risky_items.reason: The exposure registry and L80-L115 notes repeatedly warn that these labels must stay separate.

needs_orchestrator_review: Coordinate with corpus_feasibility worker before choosing concrete projects for any new family, especially exact budget/deadline and official-contact cases.
Coordinate with gate_design worker to decide which families require field-level scoring, claim-preservation gates, abstention-tail gates, or plain-language usefulness checks.
Before generating final eval JSON, decide whether the cohort is meant to be strict validation, source-inspected diagnostic, source-exposed regression, targeted repair, or measurement calibration.
Avoid reusing qv7/qv8/qv9 project/question shapes as if they were fresh; use them as pattern evidence only.

commands_run: ConvertFrom-Json summaries for selected eval/questions*.json files with UTF-8
Read selected loop reports for claim preservation, sensitive refusal-tail, plain-language quality, field-level scoring, and v9 source-inspected diagnostics
observed_results: Worker output contract requires schema parallel_team_worker_output.v1, proposal status, and directly_modified_protected_paths as an empty array.
Exposure registry shows v7, v8, v9, L108, and L109 families are diagnostic-only, while L37 remains the strict representative row.
Prior evidence supports realistic families around extraction, comparison, follow-up, exact fields, refusal boundaries, sensitive fabrication, title fragments, wrong premises, and noisy paraphrases.
No protected paths were edited by this worker; only the requested worker output JSON was created.
